mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year walk-forward test (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue forecasts and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks and factory slowdowns that compress short-term earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles, negative INDPRO shocks do not produce significantly negative 5-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 55% win rate.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, higher borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration and wage stagnation, weakening consumer purchasing power and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE increases simultaneously
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or the joint condition occurs without corresponding equity drawdowns over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that expands corporate profit margins and revenue bases, directly translating to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the relationship reverses during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading below 100.0 signals output contraction relative to the 2017 baseline, indicating reduced manufacturing capacity utilization that suppresses earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100.0, or the threshold signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recovery phases.
